intent: Verify each target failure produces a deterministic outcome on repeated runs after the BLF-01 fixes, so promotion authority can rely on the baseline as a stable starting point for H01 red-team work.

replay_sets.notes: Both runs exit 0 with the single test PASSED. No state mutation between runs.
After the fixture pin, both runs PASS regardless of host commit.gpgsign global config.
After the fixture pin, both runs PASS without GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL overrides.

After pinning behavioral pytest invocations to sys.executable, the runner produces overall_status=pass deterministically. _normalize_pytest_invocation rewrites both bare `pytest` and `python -m pytest` argv heads.

negative_replay_checks.scenario: If a future regression unpins commit.gpgsign in _init_repo, test_blf_01_baseline_gate.py::test_contract_impact_analysis_helper_pins_signing fails because the helper text no longer contains `"commit.gpgsign", "false"`.

If a future regression reverts _normalize_pytest_invocation, test_blf_01_baseline_gate.py::test_runner_pins_pytest_invocation fails because pytest argv heads are no longer rewritten to sys.executable.

If the BLF-01 artifact set is mutated to drop a target failure or weaken status, scripts/run_blf_01_baseline_gate.py exits 1 with a machine-readable reason code; corresponding unit tests in test_blf_01_baseline_gate.py also fail.
